reviews.review_msg: She is the best teacher EVER. She is an asset to this university, and deserves to be recognize. Not only does she makes math fun, she is super inspirational during her lectures, and encourages you to see math as a friend and not as an enemy. She will assign a lot of homework, not to make you angry but to get you into the habit of practicing.

Dr. Drakes is not a bad math teacher. She is funny so her lectures aren't boring. Her exams can be challenging but they are fair. She encourages questions, and is always happy to answer them. Sometimes she picks on random people to answer questions in lecture though. I didn't find her rude.. don't take her jokes too seriously.

Dr. Drakes is the most clear, straight-forward math professor at SFU.  She is very receptive to questions, and her notes are really good.  Apparently she offends some people; I think they need to lighten up.  Her exams are difficult but fair; she asks theory questions as well as math problems (you have to use words AND numbers)
there is a difference between teaching and copying answers to blackboard, and she didn't show it.
In my opinion, Dr. Drakes is a rude, tactless person. She is an ordinary instructor, definitely better than an average one, but I had no pleasure to return to the classroom.
though she went back, she is a joker more than a teacher. her class is recommended for students with joke major ONLY

Best Prof ever. You cant complain that you didnt do well on exam because it was hard. Its only because you didnt study enough for her exam. She teaches stuff so well and her notes are ever better than text book. Her final was pretty hard but she scaled one grade at the end. She is going back to Barbados. I am definitely gonna miss her class
Great teacher, but has extremely high expectations of students - very, very very difficult exams, homework and quizzes.

Her notes are concise and her lectures are lively. If you do the homework and follow her instructions, the exams will not be that hard. HINT: If you do not know how to do a question make a diagram or just put the formula down. She gives you more then 1/2  the marks for the question if you do this.

Chiaka is awesome. Her notes are concise and her lectures are lively. She will keep you awake with her humour and will encourage questions after every concept she explains. If you do the homework and follow her instructions, the exams will not be that hard. If you walk into the exam unprepared thinking you will do okay, you're gonna have a bad time

Overall average prof. Exams are harder than other math courses I've taken. She refuses to release averages and information about the exams which is annoying. I personally wouldn't recommend her over other math profs.

I miss her FUN lecture! Hard exams but she definitely want you to learn a lot and prepare for the upper level courses! Work hard and you'll be ok! Trust me you'll miss her when you are taking other math courses with boring profs

At  first, I was scared to take a math course, but her optimistic personality and encouraging teaching skills gave my hope to stop being afraid of math.  Just because the class is hard doesn't mean it's the teacher's fault.  She is one of the best math teachers in the school. I would recommend to take classes with her :)!!

Dr.Drakes is quite the amazing individual, incredibly hilarious with her sarcastic remarks that made me actually want to go to class. Midterms, quizzes, and the final were based directly form the text book. BUY THE TEXT BOOK AND DO THE QUESTIONS. If you study/prepare this way you will be guaranteed at least a B+.
Great prof, easy to understand and has clear notes, also friendly. not exactly easy but that's more course based to most people

Chiaka is hands down the best calculus professor out there. She is highly energetic and genuinely wants her students to learn. Her notes are very clear and she is available for extra help in the math lab. Taking the class with anyone else is a mistake!
Never never never never take with her. Midterms are very very hard compared to other math session. And she never give the stats of the midterm. The reason might be that the average is very low.

She is a great professor. Easy to understand, and she's funny so the class is enjoyable. However, her tests are extremely fast so be prepared to memorize questions and the procedures for them rather than understand the process and apply it.

Dr. Drakes. ohmygod. she is just so amazing I can't even use words that would justify her amazingness. The class itself is a difficult one but she does a good job teaching it, and is SUPER helpful if you ask or go to open lab when she's there.

Possibly the best math prof in history. Shows great passion for math & ALWAYS encourages questions. Weekly quizzes and midterms were completely fair, no surprises. Her sense of humor makes the class very enjoyable. Final exam was kind of lengthy but fair as well. Her notes are clear & concise and is always around for extra help. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED!

Doctor Drakes, oh, Doctor Drakes. She is seriously my favorite human being. Ever. She's clear, entertaining and helpful. Although math is probably one of my least favourite subjects, I go to class. Always. She's just so awesome! Thus far, her midterms have been fair - just a bit more time would have been nice.
AVOID AVOID AVOID Inappropriately difficult coursework. Don't take her class unless you are an actual math genious. She is funny and entertaining though.
Hard final, the hardest I've ever seen

An energetic prof who gives very helpful notes. She tries to encourage questions, keeps office hours, and promotes interest. The exams are VERY difficult; the "memory sheet" is virtually useless for the theorems she tests (nearly exact wording required). Also, her homework assignments are good practice, but quite lengthy.
soooo hard,the hardest course in the whole universe . dont take it, u will regret i guarantee.
Her exam is so hard, incredibly hard!
Nice lecturs, but Exams are so hard!!

She's very energetic and interesting to listen to, which, for a math professor, is a huge bonus. She's helpful, and encourages questions, but I find her explanations too 'mathy' for my tastes; she doesn't relate the material to the outside world or give good analogies so that you have a better understanding. Her midterm and final are brutally hard.
I done like her, she's too energetic and always talk too much
I don't like her at all !! she is funny and nice, however her class is too hard. she likes to put some tricky problems in the exams and overall her exams is too hard !!!!

She is a good professor, the midterm was a bit of a loose cannon and nobody expected the format that was given. However she basically has allowed everyone to redeem themselves for the final which is nice. Enthusiastic prof, encourages questions and participation in class!

Sfu is lucky to have her to teach math. She is really one if the best math prof I have seen in my life. She makes math so much more interesting. Very enthusiastic about math. Super engaging in class and in office hour. She says "if you are not good at math at all, drop this course" so expect to put in more efforts if you want to do good in class

I did not enjoy Chiaka's class. I found her to be condescending at times and she likes picking people out of the class to answer questions at random. She is a little too enthusiastic for me, but clearly not for others. I found her class really difficult to understand and I didn't like her teaching style.

Dr.Drakes is one of the persons who love their job! One lecture she told us that she can play with math all day and never get bored! If you are going to take math at SFU, definately take her classes! Attending her lectures is fun!

She is a really good prof, loves Math and she shows you how to do everything. Also she makes sure that she engages the class so you wont dare to fall asleep! Midterms are always require some studies out from you, so if you dont go to class or dont do her assignments then you will not do as good by just merely attending lecture.

very good lecturer, you actually LEARN STUFF from her class. The material is hard but she's a great prof to have. Always encourages questions. The course moves pretty fast though so be prepared, still she's a great teacher.
She's the best math prof I've had. She uses club references to teach us 3D calculus. Really enthusiastic and great at 8:30 in the morning. BEST PROF EVER.
She teaches really well. Can make difficult concepts easy to understand. However, sometimes it's difficult trying to hear what she is saying because she does have an accent. Overall, pretty good

Don't know where SFU found Chiaka, but SFU is damn lucky to have found a person of that calibre. Explains everything super well, and is super enthusiastic about math. Self proclaimed to talk about math all day. She may get a little judgemental if you ask her a trivial question, but she will still answer it.
Extremely enthusiastic, encourages lots of questions. Available for help after lectures, and responds promptly to emails. Best calculus prof I've had!

Fantastic professor. She makes 8:30 AM calculus less painful with her sense of humour and enthusiasm. Lots of opportunities to get help from either her or the TAs. Fantastic at explaining things that would otherwise be complicated by formal definitions.
Not easy to understand what she want to say but overall she is good
